Article.ArticleTitle: Editorial: The Reciprocal Relationship Between Sleep and Stress in Elite Athletes.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: IRCCS Istituto Ortopedico Galeazzi, Milan, Italy.
French National Institute of Sport (INSEP), Paris, France.
Institute of Sport and Preventive Medicine, Saarland University, Saarbrücken, Germany.
Appleton Institute for Behavioral Science, Central Queensland University, Rockhampton, QLD, Australia.

CommentsCorrections.RefSource: Editorial on the Research Topic The Reciprocal Relationship Between Sleep and Stress in Elite Athletes

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: The authors declare that the research was conducted in the absence of any commercial or financial relationships that could be construed as a potential conflict of interest.

Reference.Citation: Akerstedt T., Kecklund G., Axelsson J. (2007). Impaired sleep after bedtime stress and worries. Biol. Psychol. 76, 170–173. 10.1016/j.biopsycho.2007.07.010

Aloulou A., Duforez F., Léger D., De Larochelambert Q., Nedelec M. (2021). The relationships between training load, type of sport, and sleep among high-level adolescent athletes. Int. J. Sports Physiol. Perform. 16:463. 10.1123/ijspp.2020-0463

Crowley S. J., Wolfson A. R., Tarokh L., Carskadon M. A. (2018). An update on adolescent sleep: new evidence informing the perfect storm model. J. Adolesc. 67, 55–65. 10.1016/j.adolescence.2018.06.001

Filipas L., Ferioli D., Banfi G., La Torre A., Vitale J. A. (2021). Single and combined effect of acute sleep restriction and mental fatigue on basketball free-throw performance. Int. J. Sports Physiol. Perform. 16, 415–420. 10.1123/IJSPP.2020-0142

Fullagar H. H., Skorski S., Duffield R., Hammes D., Coutts A. J., Meyer T. (2015). Sleep and athletic performance: the effects of sleep loss on exercise performance, and physiological and cognitive responses to exercise. Sports Med. 45, 161–186. 10.1007/s40279-014-0260-0

Janse van Rensburg D. C., Jansen van Rensburg A., Fowler P. M., Bender A. M., Stevens D., Sullivan K. O., et al. (2021). Managing travel fatigue and jet lag in athletes: a review and consensus statement. Sports Med. 51, 2029–2050. 10.1007/s40279-021-01502-0
Kahn M., Sheppes G., Sadeh A. (2013). Sleep and emotions: bidirectional links and underlying mechanisms. Int. J. Psychophysiol. 89, 218–228. 10.1016/j.ijpsycho.2013.05.010

Nédélec M., Lienhart N., Martinent G., Doron J. (2021). Personality traits, stress appraisals and sleep in young elite athletes: a profile approach. Eur. J. Sport Sci. 21:e1829716. 10.1080/17461391.2020.1829716
Sargent C., Lastella M., Halson S. L., Roach G. D. (2021). How much sleep does an elite athlete need? Int. J. Sports Physiol. Perform. 21, 1–12. 10.1123/ijspp.2020-0896
Vandekerckhove M., Cluydts R. (2010). The emotional brain and sleep: an intimate relationship. Sleep Med. Rev. 14, 219–226. 10.1016/j.smrv.2010.01.002

Vitale J. A., Bonato M., Petrucci L., Zucca G., La Torre A., Banfi G. (2021). Acute sleep restriction affects sport-specific but not athletic performance in junior tennis players. Int. J. Sports Physiol. Perform. 19, 1–6. 10.1123/ijspp.2020-0390

Walsh N. P., Halson S. L., Sargent C., Roach G. D., Nédélec M., Gupta L., et al. (2021). Sleep and the athlete: narrative review and 2021 expert consensus recommendations. Br. J. Sports Med. 55, 356–368. 10.1136/bjsports-2020-102025
